topic: Two friends both apply for the same job
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam stared at the glossy application form, the ink seeming to mock his own hesitant handwriting. Sarah, across the small cafe table, was laughing, a bright, unrestrained sound that cut through the low hum of conversation. He traced the edge of his pen, his knuckles turning white against the cheap plastic. *She always has this effortless charm,* he thought, a tight knot forming just beneath his ribs. He reread the job description for the marketing coordinator role, the words blurring into an irritating pattern. He felt a sudden, sharp tightening in his chest, a physical pressure that made it hard to draw a full breath. He quickly shoved the paper into his bag, the sound of the zipper a small, defeated sigh.

[story 2]

"You're really going for the Senior Analyst position, aren't you?" Maya asked, leaning back in her chair, a knowing smirk playing on her lips. Alex felt a strange hollowness bloom in his stomach as he looked at her confident posture. He fiddled with the strap of his messenger bag, his gaze flicking repeatedly to the neatly printed resume lying on the desk. *It’s just… she seems so naturally suited for this,* he mused internally, a subtle downturn of his mouth betraying his internal state. He cleared his throat, trying to sound casual, but his voice came out thin. "Yeah, I think I will. It seems like a good fit."

[story 3]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, amplifying the silence in Chloe’s small apartment. She kept replaying the interview in her head—the way the hiring manager had smiled, the easy rapport established. She picked up her phone, scrolling through social media, landing on a post of Sarah celebrating a recent career milestone. A bitter taste coated the back of her throat. She stood up abruptly, pacing the cramped space between the sofa and the window. Her hands clenched into fists, the tension radiating up her arms. *Why does everything seem to gravitate toward her?* she wondered, the thought heavy and suffocating. She walked over to her desk, deliberately avoiding looking at the identical job posting she had just submitted.
